_comment: Synthetic incident files (metadata + short content excerpt). FILE-002 (customer-impact assessment) carries a sensitivity label and is restricted to incident command + SRE engineers; FILE-003 (commercial-impact summary) is restricted to incident command only — both demonstrate sensitivity-label / file-level permission trimming. FILE-001 is the shared incident doc (all).

files.name: Atlas Incident Doc.docx
files.summary: The shared Atlas payments incident document — the live running record of the outage: timeline, current status of restoration, mitigations, and open actions.
files.content_excerpt: Status: rollback to v4.6 complete; error rate at baseline; in monitoring before all-clear. Root cause: v4.7 connection-pool leak. Open: error-rate monitoring, customer-comms confirmation.

files.name: Atlas Outage — Customer-Impact Assessment.docx
files.label: Internal — Incident Confidential
files.summary: Sensitivity-labelled assessment of merchant impact during the Atlas payments outage. Quantifies affected merchants and failed transactions; pre-decisional input to the customer-comms commitment.
files.content_excerpt: Affected merchants: ~1,200. Failed transactions during the outage window: ~48,000. Recommended remediation: fee waiver for the window. Pre-decisional — not for wide distribution.

files.name: Atlas Outage — Commercial Impact (confidential).pdf
files.label: Confidential — Commercial
files.summary: Confidential commercial-impact summary of the Atlas outage for the executive team. Restricted to incident command leadership.
files.content_excerpt: [RESTRICTED — commercial] Estimated revenue exposure and contractual SLA-credit implications for the outage window.
